subject: feat(tabs): Phase 7 — TabBar adds DaisyUI tabs/tab class composition
body:
Seventh of 10 phases from the DaisyUI component-class audit. Small but
meaningful phase: TabBar already had proper ARIA roles (tablist/tab/
aria-selected), this phase adds DaisyUI's `tabs tabs-border` +
`tab tab-active` structural classes alongside the existing custom
`.tab-btn` / `.tab-btn-active` styling via class composition.

Changes:

TabBar.jsx:
- Outer wrapper gains `tabs tabs-border` DaisyUI classes alongside
  the existing `flex overflow-x-auto scrollbar-hide`. DaisyUI's tabs
  component applies its layout/border rules; our flex + overflow
  utilities handle the horizontal scroll on narrow viewports.
- Each tab button gains `tab` + `tab-active` (conditional) alongside
  our custom `tab-btn` / `tab-btn-active` classes. The custom classes
  provide the mono-font uppercase techy look; the DaisyUI classes
  provide the structural tabs behavior.
- Added `aria-label="Dashboard sections"` to the tablist container for
  screen reader context — "Dashboard sections, tablist, 6 items".
- Added a header comment documenting the composition rationale and
  rejected alternatives (pure DaisyUI tabs-lift/tabs-box have different
  typography; replacing .tab-btn with inline Tailwind would bloat the
  JSX).

Why keep .tab-btn / .tab-btn-active:

Our .tab-btn rules use:
  - font-family: var(--font-mono)
  - text-transform: uppercase
  - letter-spacing: 0.05em
  - font-size: 13px
  - font-weight: 500

And .tab-btn-active additionally applies a text-shadow glow in
var(--color-primary) that gives the active tab a distinctive "powered on"
look consistent with the dashboard's tech-focused aesthetic. DaisyUI's
default tab typography is sentence-case body font — clean and correct
for most apps, but doesn't match our visual language. Class composition
preserves both.

No CSS deletions this phase — .tab-btn / .tab-btn-active remain in
styles.css because they're not shadowing any DaisyUI class (DaisyUI uses
.tab, not .tab-btn, so they coexist).

Build verification:
  - npm run build: passes
  - DaisyUI .tabs, .tabs-border, .tab, .tab-active all present in built CSS
